The '''S-DST''' was a model of aquatic [[Destroyer/Legends|destroyer]] employed by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] around the time of the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]]. It used cutting [[Laser/Legends|lasers]] that operated as [[Energy pummel/Legends|energy pummels]] in an anti-ship role. It had a clamshell design, and was part of a line that included the [[S-DST|S-HDST]].<ref name="SW:GB" /> The [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] and [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[OOM-9/Legends|OOM-9]] used one to subjugate one of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo's]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]], the [[Gungan/Legends|Gungans]]. The destroyer [[Siege of Otoh Gunga|transported]] [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]]s and [[P-series droideka/Legends|destroyer droids]] to the Gungan [[Capital/Legends|capital]], [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]], only to find that it had been abandoned.<ref name="EG">{{StoryCite|story=End Game|book=Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|bformatted=''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' novelization, 2012 edition}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The S-DST is a vehicle used by the Trade Federation in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''. The vehicle was also included in the game's [[2002]] expansion pack, [[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns|''Clone Campaigns'']]. The S-DST also appears in the [[2012]] short story "[[End Game]]," written by [[James Luceno]] and included in the re-release of the [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|novelization]] of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]''.
